issue_title: Invalidating offline token is not working from client sessions tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

I have configured an user and public client with direct access grant. 
Then used offline token scope to retrieve access_token and refresh_token through "*/protocol/openid-connect/token" endpoint.
Now i want to invalidate/revoke offline_token using Keycloak ui , 
I tried to logout the offline session from Client details --> Sessions tab. 
![Screenshot 2024-02-26 at 11 11 20 am](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/15014026/3a093f1e-8681-4886-bf2c-11b344721a7b)

But Offline token is not invalidated, iam able to get new access_token using the offline token received earlier.

### Version

keycloak-23.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Offline token should be invalidated and should not allow to retrieve access token using the offline token

### Actual behavior

 Able to get new access_token using the offline token  which was logged out through UI

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configured an user and public client with direct access grant. 
2. Get access_token and refresh_token through "*/protocol/openid-connect/token" endpoint , using offline token scope 
3.  Logout the offline session from Keycloak UI by going to Client details --> Sessions tab. 
4.  Now  get new access token using the offline token "*/protocol/openid-connect/token" endpoint
     Able to retrieve access token using the offline token 

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Invalidating offline token is not working from client sessions tab

Closes #27275

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>

issue_title: FolderTheme does not support Locales with extensions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

translations

### Describe the bug

FolderTheme does not support Locales with extensions, such as  de-x-informal.

This is a regression, and was introduced with 748c53df7f9fb438277ce2542912004fd663ef97. The regexp `LEGAL_LOCALE` does not contain a hash, but Locale.toString produces hashes for extensions.


### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should support all Locales for bundles.

### Actual behavior

Login page shows an error when called with locales with extensions.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add de-x-informal to realm
2. Access the login page for this realm and switch language to de-x-informal
3. Login page shows as error and the backend logs an exception:

Exception:
```
    "loggerName": "org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler",
    "exception": {
      "refId": 1,
      "message": "Found illegal characters in locale or bundle name: messages_de__#x-informal",
      "exceptionType": "java.lang.RuntimeException",
      "frames": [
        {
          "line": 123,
          "method": "getMessages",
          "class": "org.keycloak.theme.FolderTheme"
        },
        {
          "class": "org.keycloak.theme.DefaultThemeManager$ExtendingTheme",
          "method": "getMessagesByLocale",
          "line": 263
        },
```


### Anything else?

I will follow up with a PR, adding the hash to `LEGAL_LOCALE` and a test case.
commit_message:
Restore support for locales with extensions (#27285)

Closes #27284

Signed-off-by: Albrecht Scheidig <albrecht.scheidig@hype.de>

issue_title: AWS JDBC driver throws ConcurrentModificationException
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Keycloak deployments utilising Aurora DB will encounter ConcurrentModificationExceptions. The root cause of this is a bug in the driver implementatioon https://github.com/awslabs/aws-advanced-jdbc-wrapper/issues/893 https://github.com/awslabs/aws-advanced-jdbc-wrapper/issues/855.

The issue can be worked around by downgrading to v2.3.1 or waiting for v.2.3.4 to be released

### Version

Nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No exceptions

Downgrade aws-advanced-jdbc-wrapper to v2.3.1

Closes #27290

issue_title: Denial of service via excessive response size from identity provider
issue_body:
### Source: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2890

Requests to identity providers have no response size limitation. Receiving a large HTTP response body leads to resource exhaustion and Keycloak crashes. For an exploit, a request must be triggered against a HTTP server under the control of an attacker, which sends a large HTTP response body.

**Requirements to exploit:**
Administrator rights, or a foreign IDP is controlled by another party

**Component affected:** 
org.keycloak.broker

**Version affected:** 
>=23.0.4

**Impact:** Low (Weakness). Considering that it requires administrative
access, or a malicious IDP controlled by another party, it should be
possible to mitigate the issue by blocking anomalously large responses, or
introducing a rate limiting in front of the Keycloak server.

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Create and start a TCP server, which sends an infinite HTTP chunked
stream (see attached reproducer).

2. Setup two realms, one as a identity provider for the other ("Keycloak OpenID Connect"). Both client and IDP contain valid configuration, except "User Info URL" of the IDP configuration, which is set to the Python server URL. The realm, which acts as IDP should contain an example user.

3. Login as example user via IDP.

Keycloak crashes with:
keycloak-1 | Terminating due to java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
keycloak-1 exited with code 3

issue_title: Potential race condition via initial-access-token count limit bypass
issue_body:
### Source: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2932

keycloak "client-registration" initial-access-token count limit (set by admin on token creation) can be bypassed when used multiple times in
parallel (race condition).

**Requirements:**
An initial access token created by the admin

**Component affected:** 
org.keycloak.client.registration

**Version affected:** 
>= 23.0.5

**Impact:**  Low (weakness). Considering that it requires administrative access, configuring client registration policies within a realm. In an event of a client abusing of an initial access token causing DDoS, it should be possible to revoke the token, or introduce mitigation mechanisms as rate-limiting.

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. On the Keycloak realm (UI) create initial-access-token
- Clients -> initial access token -> Create.
- Set a limit for the count (2 for example) -> Save.
- Copy the produced initial-access-token.
 
2. Use the initial-access-token in a client creation request
- For reference (assuming local proxy is used):

```
curl  --proxy 127.0.0.1:8080 -i -s -k -X $'POST' \

-H $'Host: keycloak.m2a.local:8443' -H $'Authorization: Bearer
<initial-access-token>' \

-H "Accept: */*" -H "content-type: application/json" --data-binary
$'{\"clientId\":\"client_1000\",\"name\":\"\",\"description\":\"\"}\x0d\x0a\x0d\x0a'
\

$'
https://keycloak.m2a.local:8443/realms/demo-realm/clients-registrations/default
'
```

3. Make multiple copies of the request with different parameters
- update the clientId (JSON parameter) in each request.
- send the requests in parallel.
- Note: burp suite community can be used for that

4. Inspect created clients
- Inspect the initial access token "Remaining count".
commit_message:
Avoid race condition when using initial-access-token

Closes #27294

issue_title: Secure Redirect URI executor issues
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

- The OIDC dynamic client registration does not work at all when secure-redirect-uris-executor is used in some client policy. This is due this condition is broken as authenticatedClient is null during OIDC dynamic client registration: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientpolicy/executor/SecureRedirectUrisEnforcerExecutor.java#L171

- When creating/updating client with some rootUrl, the redirect uris are incorrectly resolved based on the URL of authenticated client. For example when administrator updates the client `foo` in the admin console, the `authenticatedClient` would be `security-admin-console`. So redirect-uris will be resolved based on the rootUrl of `security-admin-console` instead of rootUrl of client `foo`. This is not correct as they should use rootUrl of the "target client", not the rootUrl of "authenticating client". See:
  - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientpolicy/executor/SecureRedirectUrisEnforcerExecutor.java#L174
  - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientpolicy/executor/SecureRedirectUrisEnforcerExecutor.java#L185

- The `secure-redirect-uri-executor` covers client redirect uris, but does not cover post-logout redirect uris. Those will be also good to cover to avoid cases when administrator has `secure-redirect-uri-executor` but someone is still able to register client with `post-logout-redirect-uri` like `*`, which means they can be used as open redirector.

### Version

nightly (from 2024-02-28)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Post-logout-redirect-uris are also verified when creating/updating client. And possibly also during logout.

### Actual behavior

Post-logout-redirect-uris are not verified

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Google Authenticator now supports SHA256 and SHA512
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Google Authenticator provider is not included in the list of supported authenticators if algorithm is not SHA1 and if number of digits is not 6

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e2ed9791efb3039692e18133151d473a69c0ef32/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/otp/GoogleAuthenticatorProvider.java#L25-L31

This information seems outdated since the app now works with other hash algorithms too and can generate 8 digit numbers.


### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Google Authenticator should be listed in supported authenticator apps even if 
* OTP policy sets hash algorithm to SHA256 or SHA512.
* OTP policy sets number of digits to 8.

### Actual behavior

Google Authenticator is not included in list of supported OTP authenticators if OTP policy sets hash algorithm SHA256 or  SHA512  or if digits is set to 8.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure SHA256 or SHA512 or number of digits 8 and see that Google Authenticator disappears from the list of supported authenticators. The list is displayed when user is setting up an authenticator app in the account console or when administrator configures OTP policy.

To validate that Google Authenticator still works even if it is not in the list:

1. Download latest Google Authenticator (Android app, updated in Play Store on 2023-05-18). 
2. Change Keycloak's OTP policy to SHA256 and SHA512, and number of digits to 8.
3. Setup authenticator app for user in account console, double check that hash algorithm was changed (click "Unable to scan?" to see parameters)
5. Check that OTP code can be validated successfully even when SHA256 or SHA512 is set as hash algorithm.
6. Check that OTP code can be validated successfully even when number of digits is set to 8.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Change supported criteria for Google Authenticator

List Google Authenticator as supported when
- hash algorithm is SHA256 or SHA512
- number of digits is 8

issue_title: Authz-client AuthorizationResource.getPermissions() ClassCastException
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

Retrieving permissions using keycloak-authz-client dependency

```java
var authz = AuthzClient.create(applicationContext.getBean(org.keycloak.authorization.client.Configuration.class));
var res = authz.authorization();
var req = new AuthorizationRequest();
try{
  var perm = res.getPermissions(req);
} catch (Exception e) {
  e.printStackTrace();
  throw e;
}
```

fails with this error:
```
app_1  | java.lang.ClassCastException: class org.keycloak.representations.idm.authorization.AuthorizationResponse cannot be cast to class java.util.List (org.keycloak.representations.idm.authorization.AuthorizationResponse is in unnamed module of loader 'app'; java.util.List is in module java.base of loader 'bootstrap')
app_1  |        at org.keycloak.authorization.client.resource.AuthorizationResource.getPermissions(AuthorizationResource.java:96)
...
```

The issue seems to be that `getPermissions()` first sets responseMode metadata to `"permissions"` [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f1e7c572da5008d2aec667642adb5cce89df25d6/authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/resource/AuthorizationResource.java#L93), which is then used [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f1e7c572da5008d2aec667642adb5cce89df25d6/authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/resource/AuthorizationResource.java#L121) to determine the return Type the json is unmarshalled into.  

[The intention seems to have been to create a new metadata object in case it's null](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f1e7c572da5008d2aec667642adb5cce89df25d6/authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/resource/AuthorizationResource.java#L88), but, in what looks like an obvious oversight, fails to ever set this object as the request metadata.





### Version

20.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When retrieving authorization permissions, the call should return a list of permissions

### Actual behavior

The call fails with a ClassCast exception (trace in description)

### How to Reproduce?

issue_title: Client registration policy "Allowed Protocol Mapper Types" prevents clients from self-updating via the client registration api
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

`Allowed Protocol Mapper Types` fails client self-update when the client tries to push an already assigned protocol mapper having a non whitelisted type.

`Allowed Protocol Mapper Types` should be smarter and only deny *addition* of non whitelisted protocol mapper type. Already assigned protocol mappers should be allowed even if they are not part of the whitelist.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Performing `PUT /realms/master/clients-registrations/default/foo` with the exact same representation retrieved from `GET /realms/master/clients-registrations/default/foo` should succeed.

### Actual behavior

`PUT /realms/master/clients-registrations/default/foo` fails with an http status 403 and response body `{"error":"insufficient_scope","error_description":"Policy 'Allowed Protocol Mapper Types' rejected request to client-registration service. Details: ProtocolMapper type not allowed"}`.

Looking at the Keycloak log, we can see that `Allowed Protocol Mapper Types` rejected protocol mapper `Client Host` because its type `oidc-usersessionmodel-note-mapper` is not whitelisted.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an OIDC client with `Client authentication` enabled, `Service account` enabled and client id `foo`
1. Notice that the client `dedicated` scope has at least the following protocol mappers of type `oidc-usersessionmodel-note-mapper`: `Client IP Address`, `Client Host` and `Client ID`
1. Generate a registration access token for this client
1. Using the registration access token, update the client *without any modification* . Submit the response body of `GET /realms/master/clients-registrations/default/foo` to `PUT /realms/master/clients-registrations/default/foo`.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
"Allowed Protocol Mapper Types" prevents clients from self-updating via client registration api (#27578)

closes #27558

issue_body:

### Area

### Describe the bug

i added KC_PROXY_HEADERS=xforwarded and removed KC_PROXY but then keycloak shows this error:
```
keycloak_1  | 2024-03-06 10:54:12,177 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Failed to start server in (production) mode
keycloak_1  | 2024-03-06 10:54:12,177 ERROR [org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) ERROR: Key material not provided to setup HTTPS. Please configure your keys/certificates or start the server in development mode.
```

but then keycloak warns:
```
The following used options or option values are DEPRECATED and will be removed in a future release:
keycloak_1  |   - proxy: Use proxy-headers.
```

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

desired setup: let reverse proxy (nginx in my case) do tls decryption and forward traffic unencrypted to docker container.
configured with some environment variables activated for reverse proxy config:

issue_title: Mixed use of javax and jakarta in org.keycloak.admin.client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

adapter/jee

### Describe the bug

Hi, we recently upgraded our java application from using Keycloak 21 to 22 and we started to have runtime exceptions related to jakarta. The package we use is `org.keycloak:keycloak-admin-client:22.0.5`

After some searching, I eventually came to this [file](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/release/22.0/integration/admin-client-jee/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/client/resource/UserProfileResource.java), where there is inconsistent reference of `APPLICATION_JSON`:

In L42

```@Consumes(jakarta.ws.rs.core.MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)```

But in L46, simply
```@Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)```

And there is no import of jakarta in the admin-client.

22.0.5

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No build issues or runtime issues when having `import org.keycloak.admin.client.Keycloak;`  

### Actual behavior

We see build error: `class file for jakarta.ws.rs.core.Response not found`.

### How to Reproduce?

(I'm not able to provide the code as it is private. But I hope I already found the root cause.)

NOTE: This issue describes 2 independent bugs.

- The part related with authentication flows was fixed by other issue #23980 .
- The part related to "email settings" configuration is related to user profile. I am leaving this issue related only to email configuration (user profile) and hence moving component to user-profile as the part related to "authentication flows" was addressed by #23980 .

issue_title: Account Console REST API for /linked-accounts Returns Multiple Access-Control-Allow-Origin Headers
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

In version 23.0.7, if I send a GET request to realms/[realm]/account/linked-accounts with Origin set, I get two Access-Control-Allow-Origin in the response.

Example:
GET  realms/[realm]/account/linked-accounts
Authentication: Bearer e...
Origin: http://localhost:4200

In the response I get the following access control headers:
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:4200
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:4200
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true

Chrome (and probably other browsers) reject this due to CORS violations:
Access to fetch at 'https://.../realms/[realm]/account/linked-accounts' from origin 'http://localhost:4200' has been blocked by CORS policy: The 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header contains multiple values 'http://localhost:4200, http://localhost:4200', but only one is allowed. Have the server send the header with a valid value, or, if an opaque response serves your needs, set the request's mode to 'no-cors' to fetch the resource with CORS disabled.

This prevents cross-origin access to the linked-accounts endpoint. This is mostly relevant for local development of the account console theme. But could also effect other users of the account API.

I looked into the code but could not directly find the cause for this. I suspect multiple places colling "addHeader.accept(ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_ORIGIN, origin);" to add the origin.


### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only one access control header is returned, so that API is callable from a frontend application.

### Actual behavior

Two access control headers are returned.

### How to Reproduce?

Set the Web Origin for the account-console client to http://localhost:4200.
Call the /linked-accounts API with an Origin header set to http://localhost:4200.
Observe two Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers in the response.


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Quarkus-next build failure: Could not find artifact io.quarkus:quarkus-extension-maven-plugin
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ci

### Describe the bug

In order to stabilize the quarkus-next branch nightly builds, we need to prevent a failure during the CI workflow build stage. It happens because of the io.quarkus:quarkus-extension-maven-plugin:jar:999-SNAPSHOT artifact is not being found [1].

Based on a local analysis, adding the Quarkus Sonatype Snapshots repository as a plugin repository could solve the problem.

[1] https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/8180607544/job/22390615429

### Version

upstream/main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Quarkus-next nigthly workflow build success.

### Actual behavior

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/8180607544/job/22390615429

### How to Reproduce?

Run the current quarkus-next branch.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Stabilize the quarkus-next build stage (#27684)

* edited prepare-quarkus-next.sh in order to add the Quarkus nightly snapshot repository as a plugin repository to avoid multiple "artifacts are not being found" failures

Closes #27683

Signed-off-by: Peter Zaoral <pzaoral@redhat.com>

issue_title: Unable to set a newly created flow in First Login Flow override for a SAML identity provider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I am trying to create a flow that will be used as First Login Flow for certain identity providers, but if I create a flow it doesn't show up in the selector of flows in identity provider configuration view.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Any flow present in "Authentication" page can be selected as First Login Flow override in identity provider configuration view.

### Actual behavior

The flows that existed before (in my case 23.0.7 version) are present in First Login Flow selector list, but newly created flows do not appear in the selector list.

The help tooltip next to the selector is also not showing the text (compared to 23.0.7 version).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a flow in "Authentication" page.
2. Go to "Identity providers" -> \<some SAML identity provider\> -> open selector next to "First login flow override" label.
3. The flow created at step 1 doesn't show up in the list.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix help key for firstBrokerLoginFlowAliasOverrideHelp (#28257)

Closes #27691

Signed-off-by: Stan Silvert <ssilvert@redhat.com>

issue_title: MTLS Cache options should be runtime options, not build time options
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The options for configuring MTLS are used only at runtime. As there they contain also a password, it is even more important to make them runtime only

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

MTLS for the internal communication options should be runtime options only 

### Actual behavior

They are build time options

### How to Reproduce?

They are listed in the build time help

### Anything else?

I'll provide a PR
commit_message:
MTLS Cache options should be runtime options, not build time options

Closes #27701

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
